title: And, bhikkhus, what is a person with expansive wisdom? Here, bhikkhus, a person goes to the monastery and often hears the Dhamma from the bhikkhus.…
content: And, bhikkhus, what is a person with expansive wisdom? Here, bhikkhus, a person goes to the monastery and often hears the Dhamma from the bhikkhus. The bhikkhus teach him the Dhamma, beautiful in the beginning, beautiful in the middle, beautiful in the end, with meaning and phrasing, revealing the completely perfected and purified holy life. While sitting there, he attends to the beginning of that discourse, attends to the middle, attends to the end. Likewise, when he rises from that seat, he attends to the beginning, attends to the middle, attends to the end. Just as, bhikkhus, an upright pot retains the water poured into it and does not pour it out, so too, bhikkhus, here a person goes to the monastery... attends to the end. Likewise, when he rises from that seat, he attends to the beginning... attends to the end. This, bhikkhus, is called a person with expansive wisdom.

title: Venerable Jatukaṇṇī: Having heard of the hero not attached to sensual pleasures, I have come here to ask the desireless one Who has crossed the…
content:
Venerable Jatukaṇṇī:
Having heard of the hero not attached to sensual pleasures,
I have come here to ask the desireless one
Who has crossed the flood, born with the (wisdom-)eye.
Please tell the path of peace — let the Blessed One truly explain it to me.

Having conquered sensual desires, the Blessed One dwells in the world,
Like the sun illuminating the earth with radiance.
O one of vast wisdom, teach me the Dhamma,
So that I, of little wisdom, may know it
And here abandon birth and old age.

The Blessed One replied to Jatukaṇṇī:
Subdue greed for sensual pleasures;
See renunciation as security.
Do not grasp or abandon anything in the world.

Dry up what is before you; have nothing after.
If you do not grasp in the middle, you will live peacefully.

O brahmin, for one completely without greed for name-and-form,
Without taints — because of these taints one is overcome by death.

title: About the downfall of a person, I ask you, Gotama. Having come to ask the Blessed One, tell me the cause of downfall. It is…
content:
About the downfall of a person, I ask you, Gotama.
Having come to ask the Blessed One, tell me the cause of downfall.

It is easy to know a prosperous person, easy to know one who is down.
One who loves the Dhamma prospers; one who hates the Dhamma declines.

One who loves the bad and not the good,
Who approves of the bad — that is the cause of downfall.

One who is fond of sleep, fond of company, lazy, of little energy,
Given to anger — that is the cause of downfall.

title: Elder Khandasumana: Having given up one flower, I enjoyed eighty thousand years in heaven, And the remaining five years I have lived at peace.
content:
Elder Khandasumana:

Having given up one flower, I enjoyed eighty thousand years in heaven,
And the remaining five years I have lived at peace.

title: Friends, what is the development of concentration that, when developed and frequently practiced, leads to mindfulness and clear comprehension? Friends, here, a bhikkhu knows the…
content: Friends, what is the development of concentration that, when developed and frequently practiced, leads to mindfulness and clear comprehension? Friends, here, a bhikkhu knows the arising of feelings, knows their persisting, knows their passing away; knows the arising of perceptions, knows their persisting, knows their passing away; knows the arising of thoughts, knows their persisting, knows their passing away. Friends, this is the development of concentration that, when developed and frequently practiced, leads to mindfulness and clear comprehension.

title: So too, bhikkhus, in the future, there will be bhikkhus. Those suttas spoken by the Tathāgata, profound, deeply meaningful, transcendent, dealing with emptiness, they will…
content: So too, bhikkhus, in the future, there will be bhikkhus. Those suttas spoken by the Tathāgata, profound, deeply meaningful, transcendent, dealing with emptiness, they will not listen to when they are being taught; they will not lend an ear; they will not apply their minds to true knowledge; and they will not think that those teachings are worth learning and mastering. But those suttas that are composed by poets, beautiful verses with beautiful phrases, belonging to outsiders, spoken by disciples, they will listen to when they are being taught; they will lend an ear; they will apply their minds to true knowledge; and they will think that those teachings are worth learning and mastering. Thus, bhikkhus, those suttas spoken by the Tathāgata, profound, deeply meaningful, transcendent, dealing with emptiness, will disappear. Therefore, bhikkhus, you should train thus: 'Those suttas spoken by the Tathāgata, profound, deeply meaningful, transcendent, dealing with emptiness — we will listen to when they are being taught; we will lend an ear; we will apply our minds to true knowledge; and we will think that those teachings are worth learning and mastering.' Thus, bhikkhus, you should train.

title: The Blessed One replied to Bhadrāvudha: Subdue all grasping and craving Above, below, across, and in between. Whoever grasps anything in the world, Because of…
content:
The Blessed One replied to Bhadrāvudha:
Subdue all grasping and craving
Above, below, across, and in between.
Whoever grasps anything in the world,
Because of that, Māra follows that person.

Therefore, a wise person, not grasping anything,
A bhikkhu mindful in the entire world,
Having no possessions, does not long.
Behold these people who grasp,
In Māra's domain, attached and clinging.

title: - What do you think, bhikkhus, which is greater: the few Simsapā leaves I have taken in my hand, or the leaves in the Simsapā…
content: - What do you think, bhikkhus, which is greater: the few Simsapā leaves I have taken in my hand, or the leaves in the Simsapā forest?" - "Venerable Sir, the few Simsapā leaves the Blessed One has taken in his hand are very few, and the leaves in the Simsapā forest are far more numerous." - "So too, bhikkhus, what I have directly known but not taught to you is far more numerous; what I have taught you is very little. But why, bhikkhus, have I not taught those things? Because they are not connected with the goal, not fundamental to the holy life, and do not lead to disenchantment, dispassion, cessation, peace, direct knowledge, enlightenment, Nibbāna. Therefore, I have not taught them. And what, bhikkhus, have I taught? 'This is suffering,' bhikkhus, I have taught. 'This is the origin of suffering,' I have taught. 'This is the cessation of suffering,' I have taught. 'This is the path leading to the cessation of suffering,' I have taught. And why, bhikkhus, have I taught these things? Because they are connected with the goal, fundamental to the holy life, and lead to disenchantment, dispassion, cessation, peace, direct knowledge, enlightenment, Nibbāna. Therefore, I have taught them.

title: There are these two things, bhikkhus, that are components of true knowledge. What two? Serenity and insight. When serenity is developed, bhikkhus, what benefit is…
content: There are these two things, bhikkhus, that are components of true knowledge. What two? Serenity and insight. When serenity is developed, bhikkhus, what benefit is to be expected? The mind is developed. When the mind is developed, what benefit is to be expected? Greed is abandoned. When insight is developed, bhikkhus, what benefit is to be expected? Wisdom is developed. When wisdom is developed, what benefit is to be expected? Ignorance is abandoned. Defiled by greed, bhikkhus, the mind is not liberated. Defiled by ignorance, wisdom is not developed. Therefore, through the removal of greed, there is liberation of mind; through the removal of ignorance, there is liberation by wisdom.

title: Master Gotama, just as among root fragrances, black sandalwood is considered supreme; among heartwood fragrances, red sandalwood is considered supreme; among flower fragrances, jasmine is…
content: Master Gotama, just as among root fragrances, black sandalwood is considered supreme; among heartwood fragrances, red sandalwood is considered supreme; among flower fragrances, jasmine is considered supreme. So too, the instruction of Master Gotama is considered supreme among instructions today.
